title: Marrakesh Agreement Establishing the WTO — Article 6_oth (Other Practice)

1 ARTICLE VI OF THE MARRAKESH AGREEMENT ESTABLISHING THE WORLD TRADE
ORGANIZATION
1.1 Text of Article VI
Article VI
The Secretariat
1. There shall be a Secretariat of the WTO (hereinafter referred to as "the
Secretariat") headed by a Director-General.
2. The Ministerial Conference shall appoint the Director-General and adopt
regulations setting out the powers, duties, conditions of service and term of office of the
Director-General.
3. The Director-General shall appoint the members of the staff of the Secretariat and
determine their duties and conditions of service in accordance with regulations adopted
by the Ministerial Conference.
4. The responsibilities of the Director-General and of the staff of the Secretariat shall
be exclusively international in character. In the discharge of their duties, the Director-
General and the staff of the Secretariat shall not seek or accept instructions from any
government or any other authority external to the WTO. They shall refrain from any
action which might adversely reflect on their position as international officials. The
Members of the WTO shall respect the international character of the responsibilities of
the Director-General and of the staff of the Secretariat and shall not seek to influence
them in the discharge of their duties.
1.2 Article VI:1
1.2.1 WTO Secretariat
1. The WTO Secretariat is based in Geneva, Switzerland and is headed by a Director-General.
2. Because the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade 1947 (GATT 1947) was applied
provisionally1, the secretariat services for the GATT 1947 were provided by the Secretariat of the
1 See GATT Analytical Index, pp. 1086-1087.
1

WTO ANALYTICAL INDEX
WTO Agreement – Article VI (Practice)
Interim Commission for the International Trade Organization (ICITO).2 The Agreement on the
Transfer of Assets, Liabilities, Records, Staff and Functions from the Interim Commission of the
International Trade Organization and the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) to the
World Trade Organization (WTO) provided that "the staff of the ICITO shall perform the duties of
the Secretariat of the WTO until the appointment of the staff of the Secretariat of the WTO". The
same Agreement also provided that the ICITO would be dissolved as of the date on which the
members of the Secretariat were appointed.3 Until an independent pension plan could be established
for the WTO Secretariat, the ICITO remained in existence so that the ICITO staff could continue to
participate in the United Nations Joint Staff Pension Fund.4
3. On 16 October 1998, the General Council adopted a Decision on Conditions of Service
Applicable to the Staff of the WTO Secretariat, including Staff Regulations and Rules and the
Regulations and Rules of the WTO Pension Plan. This Decision provided, inter alia, that "the WTO
Secretariat shall be established on 1 January 1999."5
1.3 Article VI:2
1.3.1 "[T]he Ministerial Conference shall appoint the Director-General"
4. The General Council has appointed the following Directors-General to date:
a. Mr Peter Sutherland – from 1 January 1995 to 30 April 19956;
b. Mr Renato Ruggiero – from 1 May 1995 to 30 April 19997;
c. Mr Mike Moore – from 1 September 1999 to 31 August 20028;
d. Dr Supachai Panitchpakdi – from 1 September 2002 to 31 August 20059;
e. Mr Pascal Lamy – from 1 September 2005 to 31 August 2009, reappointed for a second
four-year term starting 1 September 200910;
f. Mr Roberto Carvalho de Azevêdo – from 1 September 2013 to 31 August 2017 and
reappointed for a second four-year term starting 1 September 201711, which Mr Azevêdo
served until 31 August 202012; and
g. Dr. Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala – from 1 March 2021 to 31 August 2025.13
2 GATT Analytical Index, pp. 1122-1123.
3 PC/9 − L/7580 (with the draft text of the agreement); approved in ICITO/1/39 and WT/GC/M/1; and
WT/L/36 (with the text of the agreement).
4 See paragraph 8 below.
5 WT/L/282.
6 Mr Sutherland, as the former Director-General to the GATT 1947, served as the first Director-General
pursuant to Article XVI:2 of the WTO Agreement.
7 Appointed at the General Council meeting of 24 March 1995 (see WT/GC/M/2, p. 1).
8 Appointed at the General Council meeting of 22 July 1999 (see WT/GC/M/46, p. 18; text of decision,
WT/L/308).
9 Also appointed at the meeting of 22 July 1999, to succeed to Mr Moore (see WT/GC/M/46, p. 18; text
of decision, WT/L/308).
10 Appointed at the meeting of 26 May 2005 (WT/GC/M/95, item 9); reappointed at the meeting of
29-30 April 2009 (WT/GC/M/119).
11 Appointed at the meeting of 14 May 2013 (WT/GC/M/144, item 1); reappointed at the meeting of
27 February 2017 (WT/GC/M/166, item 1).
12 https://www.wto.org/english/news_e/news20_e/dgra_14may20_e.htm (see WT/GC/M/184, item 5,
para 5.1).
13 Appointed at the meeting of 15 February 2021 (WT/GC/M/189, item 1, para 1.11). As regards the
period between 1 September 2020 and 14 February 2021, see WT/GC/M/186, item 6, para. 6.13.
2

WTO ANALYTICAL INDEX
WTO Agreement – Article VI (Practice)
1.3.2 "[R]egulations setting out the powers, duties, conditions of service and term of
office of the Director-General"
5. At its meeting of 22 July 1999, the General Council resolved that, "in order to improve and
strengthen the current rules and procedures [for the appointment of the Director-General], a
comprehensive set of rules and procedures for such appointments shall be elaborated and adopted
by the end of September 2000."14 The General Council approved a comprehensive set of procedures
for the appointment of the Director-General at its meeting on 10-12 and 20 December 2002.15 These
procedures have been applied in subsequent selection and appointment processes for the
Director-General.16
1.4 Article VI:3
1.4.1 "[D]uties and conditions of service" of the WTO Secretariat
1.4.1.1 Staff Regulations and Rules
6. The General Council adopted decisions regarding the terms of service applicable to the WTO
staff at its meetings of 30 October 1995; 7, 8 and 13 November 1996; 7 February 1997; 30 June
1997 and 1 July 1997; and 24 April 1998.17 The General Council further established a Working Group
on Conditions of Service Applicable to the Staff of the WTO Secretariat18 on 7 February 1997.
7. On 16 October 1998, taking into consideration the report of the Working Group, the General
Council adopted the proposed Staff Regulations and Staff Rules.19 The General Council agreed to
amend the Staff Rules at its meetings of 8 and 9 February 200120; 25, 26 and 30 August 200321;
17-18 May 200422; 13 December 200423; 28 July 200924; 30 November 201125; 5 May 201526;
27-28 July 201527; 12 May 201628; 26 July 201729; 30 November 201730; 26 July 201831; and
25 November 2020.32
1.4.1.2 WTO Pension Plan
8. The ICITO staff participated in the United Nations Joint Staff Pension Fund (UNJSPF).33
On 16 October 1998, consequent to agreement on the conditions of service applicable to the staff
and on establishment of an independent WTO Pension Plan (WTOPP), the Executive Committee of
ICITO and the General Council decided to terminate the membership of ICITO in the UNJSPF on
14 WT/L/308, last paragraph.
15 WT/GC/M/77; WT/L/509. In addition, the General Council approved modified Conditions of Service for
the Director-General, issued as WT/GC/67.
16 See WT/GC/M/95, item 9 and WT/GC/M/119 (appointment and reappointment of Director-General
Lamy); WT/GC/M/144, item 1 and WT/GC/M/166 (appointment and reappointment of Director-General
Azevêdo); and WT/GC/M/189, item 1, para 1.11 (appointment of Director-General Okonjo-Iweala).
17 WT/GC/M/7, item 1, decision in WT/L/91; WT/GC/M/16, item 6, decision in WT/L/197; WT/GC/M/18,
item 3, decision in WT/L/205; WT/GC/M/20, item 1, decision in WT/L/223; and WT/GC/M/28, item 1, decision
in WT/L/269.
18 WT/L/205.

Regulations, Staff Rules and the Regulations and Administrative Rules of the WTO Pension Plan as adopted.
20 WT/GC/M/63, item 3, amendments in WT/BFA/W/48/Rev.1 (performance award programme).
21 WT/GC/N/82, item 2, amendments in WT/BFA/W/102.
22 WT/GC/M/86, amendments in WT/BFA/W/112.

24 WT/GC/M/121, item 9, amendments in WT/BFA/W/185.

26 WT/GC/M/157, item 8, amendments in WT/BFA/W/353 and Corr.1 (see WT/BFA/148, item 2).
27 WT/GC/M/158, item 11, amendments in WT/BFA/W/360 (see WT/BFA/149, item 9).
28 WT/GC/M/162, item 4, amendments in WT/BFA/153, item 10.
29 WT/GC/M/168, item 11, amendments in WT/BFA/W/416/Rev.3 (see WT/BFA/164/Rev.1).
30 WT/GC/M/170, item 11, amendments in WT/BFA/169.
31 WT/GC/M/173, item 9, amendments in WT/BFA/W/448 (see WT/BFA/172, item I, para 1.12).
32 WT/GC/M/187, item 11, amendments in WT/BFA/W/524 (see WT/BFA/188, item 6, para 6.8).
33 GATT Analytical Index, p. 1123.
3

WTO ANALYTICAL INDEX
WTO Agreement – Article VI (Practice)
31 December 1998. The General Council also decided to adopt the WTOPP Regulations and
Administrative Rules.34
9. The Regulations of the WTO Pension Plan were amended by the General Council on
1 December 2005, on 28 July 2009, on 26 October 2011, on 30 November 2015, and on
26 July 2017.35 The Administrative Rules were amended on 23 September 2005 and on
7 December 2016, and the Rules of Procedure were amended on 7 December 2016.36
10. The General Council has approved agreements on the transfer of pension rights between the
WTOPP and the UNJSPF37, the WTOPP and the pension scheme of the OECD38, the WTOPP and the
pension schemes of the Coordinated Organizations39, and the WTOPP and the European Communities
Pension Scheme.40
11. The WTO Pension Plan Management Board presents a report each year to the General Council
and to the participants, as per Art. 5(d) of the Regulations of the WTO Pension Plan.
1.5 Article VI:4: responsibilities and conduct of Director-General and Secretariat staff
12. See the Staff Regulations and Staff Rules of the WTO.41
___

WT/GC/M/133; WT/GC/W/706; WT/GC/M/160; WT/GC/M/168; and WT/L/1010.

(EUMETSAT), European Institute for Securities Studies (ISS) and the European Union Satellite Centre
(SatCen).
